ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Chinese stir-fried shredded potato; closest parent 'potato food product' (FOODON_03301710) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Potato products and dishes) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for Chinese shredded potato stir-fry exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 735248001 'Potato dish (food)' is the closest parent; no exact code for crisp shredded potato stir-fry exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of crisp shredded potato is approximately 200 g, presented as a mound of julienned potato strips lightly dressed with vinegar and chilli oil.

nutrient_source.title: Potatoes, boiled, cooked in skin, flesh, without salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170026); adapted for stir-fried shredded potato with oil and seasoning using USDA Foundation Foods raw potato (FDC ID 170032) and standard stir-fry oil addition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Raw potato values (USDA SR Legacy FDC 170032) used as the base. A standard stir-fry oil addition of approximately 5 g vegetable oil per 100 g cooked dish is applied, increasing fat and energy. Sodium reflects light seasoning (salt, soy-free). Values represent the cooked dish per 100 g edible portion. This is the best available approved-source approximation; no Singapore HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry for this specific dish was identified.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — boiled/stir-fried potato analogous estimate; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chinese stir-fried shredded potato (土豆丝). GI of 56 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled white potato (GI ~56–59, medium category; Atkinson et al. 2021). Cold-rinsing and vinegar addition in this dish may lower the effective GI slightly via starch retrogradation and acetic acid effects; GI 56 is therefore a conservative upper-bound estimate. GL per serving = (56 × 13.5 × 200/100) / 100 = 15.1.

hypertension.guidance: A 200 g restaurant serving provides approximately 420 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt and avoid additional soy sauce or chilli paste to keep daily sodium within the recommended 2000 mg limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and starch load from potato
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 56 and a glycemic load of ~15 per serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150 g and pair the dish with non-starchy vegetables and lean protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response; the vinegar dressing may modestly attenuate glycemic rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and absence of cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is low in saturated fat (~0.5 g per 100 g) and contains no cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly vegetable side dish when prepared with minimal oil; potassium content (~340 mg per 100 g) supports vascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crisp Shredded Potato (清爽土豆丝, qīngshuǎng tǔdòu sī) is a popular Chinese home-style and restaurant dish consisting of raw potato that is julienned into fine matchstick strips, briefly rinsed in cold water to remove excess starch, and then rapidly stir-fried in a wok over high heat with garlic, dried chilli, rice vinegar, and salt. The cold-water rinse and vinegar are the defining techniques that give the dish its characteristic crisp, slightly crunchy texture rather than the soft, floury texture of fully cooked potato. The flavour profile is tangy, mildly spicy, and savoury. The dish is served as a side vegetable accompaniment in Chinese cuisine, commonly found in both home kitchens across northern and central China and in Chinese restaurants throughout Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides approximately 98 kcal, 1.8 g protein, 4.5 g fat (predominantly unsaturated from vegetable oil), and 13.5 g carbohydrate with 1.4 g dietary fibre. The glycemic index is estimated at 56 (medium), and the glycemic load per 200 g restaurant serving is approximately 15. Sodium content (~210 mg per 100 g) is moderate and depends heavily on seasoning. The dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is a reasonable choice for cardiovascular health due to its low saturated fat content, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size given the moderate starch load, and those with hypertension should request reduced salt preparation.
common_user_queries: How many calories in crisp shredded potato?
What is the GI of potato stir-fry?
What does 清爽土豆丝 mean?
How is tu dou si made?

confidence_reason: No exact match for this specific dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central raw potato data (SR Legacy) with a standard stir-fry oil addition, which is an approved secondary global source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using boiled white potato as the closest defensible reference. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy raw potato (FDC 170032) with an estimated 5 g vegetable oil addition per 100 g cooked dish; this is the best available approved-source approximation. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero for vegetable-oil-based stir-fry with no hydrogenated fat). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; this is a plant-based dish). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 (confirmed zero; white potato contains negligible beta-carotene). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero; no fortified or animal-derived ingredients). 3. GI of 56 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (boiled white potato); vinegar addition may lower effective GI in practice. GL per 200 g serving = (56 × 13.5 × 200/100) / 100 = 15.1. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy potato data and are not -1 as the source reports these values. 5. Price data (SGD 15.34) from field survey data (See Ya Tonight restaurant) is preserved as context but is not used as a nutritional source. 6. The dish name in the input (清爽土豆丝) uses 清爽 meaning 'refreshing/crisp', which is a restaurant-specific descriptor; the canonical dish category is 土豆丝 (shredded potato stir-fry).
